title: 7 Central 1 High Interest Savings Accounts
body_text:
7	Central 1 High Interest Savings Accounts
7.1	Overview of Central 1 High Interest Savings Accounts
The Central 1 High Interest Savings Account (HISA) is a low-risk deposit investment option for your financial institution. A HISA helps you earn more on surplus funds while retaining access to liquidity. Authorized users view and manage your HISA via Treasury Connect. See Section 7.3, Viewing, Funding and Redeeming Funds from a High Interest Savings Account (HISA) via Treasury Connect for more information. HISAs are in Canadian dollars.
7.2	Opening and Closing a High Interest Savings Account (HISA)
Open a HISA as follows:
Complete High Interest Savings Account Agreement, Form 10133. See Figure 1. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Figure 1: High Interest Savings Account Application, Form 10133 â Page 1
Have two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Forward the form to Central 1 as directed.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Central 1 notifies you when your HISA is ready for use.
To close your HISA, contact Central 1âs Treasury team at
treasury@central1.com
.
Related Information
High Interest Savings Account Application,
Form 10133
7.3	Viewing, Funding and Redeeming Funds from a High Interest Savings Account (HISA) via Treasury Connect
Authorized users view and manage your HISA and other Central 1 accounts via Treasury Connect. See Treasury Connect User Guide, Form 6444, for procedures. Users require the Treasury Connect application assigned to the Client Centre ID and the following permissions as appropriate:
Account Summary
â Allows authorized users to view your Central 1 accounts via Treasury Connect
Transfer Funds
â Allows users to transfer funds between your Central 1 accounts (including your HISA).
Viewing your HISA Via Treasury Connect
Authorized users at your financial institution can view your Central 1 account summary and transaction information, view and export posting account activity, and view your account rates via Treasury Connect.
The High Interest Savings Account (HISA) displays separately from your Canadian dollar current account. HISAs are in Canadian dollars.
Account data is updated throughout the day in near real time, as transactions are processed by our banking system. Opening balances, (including overnight settlement transactions), are generally available by 5:30 am PT (8:30 am ET). A 7-year transaction history is available.
Funding Your HISA Via Treasury Connect
To fund your HISA, authorized users must transfer funds from one of your Central 1 Canadian dollar accounts via Treasury Connect.
Important
:	Funds sent to the HISA transit from an external account will be returned. HISA transits arenât issued by or registered with Payments Canada; therefore, they canât be used to send or receive external funds transfers.
You can contribute to your HISA in any increment, up to a maximum of $10,000,000.00 CAD. If you exceed the maximum amount, Treasury Connect wonât allow the transfer to proceed.
Redeeming Funds From Your HISA Via Treasury Connect
To redeem funds from your HISA, authorized users must transfer funds to one of your Canadian dollar accounts in Treasury Connect. Redemption from your HISA must be scheduled for a future date. You can schedule a redemption from your HISA a minimum of 31 calendar days to a maximum of 90 calendar days from the date you initiate the request.
You can redeem funds in any increment, up to the balance in your HISA account at the time you initiate the redemption request. HISA redemptions are processed on business days only. If you initiate a redemption for a non-business day (e.g., a statutory holiday), the transfer of funds will occur on the following business day.
Note:	Scheduled HISA redemptions canât be cancelled or reversed.
Related Information
Treasury connect User Guide,
Form 6444
END
